commit.message:
Fix: graceful fallback when starred/expires_at columns missing

- GET /messages: if starred/expires_at columns don't exist, retries
  with base columns only so messages always load
- POST /send: if expires_at insert fails due to missing column, retries
  without it so messages without expiry always save correctly
- PATCH /messages/star: returns clear error message if column missing

Root cause: ALTER TABLE migration not yet run in Supabase. These fixes
make the app work correctly before migration is run and after.

Complete Phase 5.2: Texture Atlas Management

- Add mmap/munmap C ABI functions in Rust (render-sys/src/lib.rs)
  * buffer_mmap(): Maps GPU buffers into CPU address space via i915_gem_mmap_offset
  * buffer_munmap(): Unmaps previously mapped GPU buffers

- Add Go bindings for mmap operations (internal/render/dmabuf.go)
  * BufferHandle.Mmap(): Returns byte slice pointing to mapped GPU memory
  * BufferHandle.Munmap(): Unmaps and syncs changes to GPU

- Implement font atlas GPU upload (internal/render/atlas/atlas.go)
  * UploadFontAtlas() now uses mmap to copy SDF data to GPU buffer
  * Validates data size and handles errors gracefully

- Implement image atlas GPU upload (internal/render/atlas/atlas.go)
  * UploadImageData() uses mmap for row-by-row pixel copy
  * Correctly handles page stride and region offsets
  * Updates LRU counter on each upload

- Implement LRU eviction (internal/render/atlas/atlas.go)
  * evictLRURegions(): Evicts 25% of least-recently-used regions when full
  * getRegionsSortedByLRU(): Helper for LRU-sorted region IDs
  * Integrated into AllocateImageRegion() when all pages are full

- Implement shelf space reuse (internal/render/atlas/atlas.go)
  * removeRegionFromShelf(): Removes freed regions from shelf lists
  * Enables space reclamation for future allocations

- Tests: All 9 atlas tests passing, all 185 Rust tests passing
- Zero functional regressions in existing code
- Ready for Phase 5.3: Double/Triple Buffering

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
rpc: Fix uptime always showing 0 - compute current session uptime

total_uptime is only accumulated on disconnect, so nodes that have been
online continuously since launch always showed 0. Both masternodelist
and masternode_status now return total_uptime + (now - uptime_start)
when the node is active, giving the true elapsed uptime.

feat: sync masternode.conf when entries are saved/deleted/imported

When a masternode entry is saved, deleted, or imported via the wallet UI,
the wallet now writes all current entries to masternode.conf in the wallet
directory (~/.time-wallet/testnet/masternode.conf for testnet).

giving users a human-readable config file that the timed daemon can also

- src/common/ — shared constants, config

- src/inject/ — injected page scripts
- src/ui/ — everything React

commit.message:
fix(ci): construct ECS task definitions from spec instead of fetching from AWS

Previously the deploy steps called describe-task-definition and mutated
the result. This meant the pipeline couldn't run until terraform apply
had already registered the task definitions — breaking fresh environments
and the post-EKS→ECS migration where no task defs existed yet.

New approach: build the full task definition JSON inline with jq -n,
deriving all environment-specific values from the cluster name:
- IAM role ARNs: constructed from account ID + cluster name
- SSM parameter paths: /ticket-platform/{env}/... (env stripped from cluster)
- CloudWatch log group: /ecs/{cluster}/api|frontend

This mirrors the terraform spec exactly so task defs registered by the
pipeline are identical to what terraform would create. No describe-task-
definition call needed — works on first deploy before terraform has run,
and registers a clean new revision on every push.

Also fixes ecs:ListClusters IAM permission — it requires Resource '*'
but was incorrectly placed in the ECSUpdateService statement which
uses specific resource ARNs. Moved to the ECSRegisterTaskDef statement.

* why...

* Fix pool usage, enable parallel analyzer, fix data races

- Add Parallel.run_with_pool to wrap operations in Task.run (fixes
  Unhandled(Wait) exception from missing effect handler)
- Update all call sites to use run_with_pool instead of bare Lazy.force
- Enable parallel analyzer by default (remove opt-in gate)

Co-authored-by: Simn <634365+Simn@users.noreply.github.com>

* move apply_params_stack to uctx

* kinda works

* add DomainMutex

* Fix DomainMutex data race and thread ID generation race

The DomainMutex.downer field was a non-atomic mutable record field read by
multiple domains without synchronization. In OCaml 5, this is a data race
that can cause a domain to see a stale ownership value, incorrectly thinking
it owns the mutex. Fixed by using Atomic.t for the owner ID field.

Also fixed thread ID generation which used non-atomic Atomic.incr + Atomic.get
pattern. Replaced with atomic Atomic.fetch_and_add.

Co-authored-by: Simn <634365+Simn@users.noreply.github.com>

* pull changes from sub-PR

* investigate if we're actually looking at the problem

* revert

* focus

* focus more

* pull luv changes to get that out of the way

* pull ThreadSafeHashtbl changes because that's a clear bugfix

* pull Deque changes because that's simpler

* use normal mutex for condition because it doesn't have to be reentrant

* enable hanging test again to see how it behaves on other OS

* fix thread id in debug

* don't use Sys.time for Lock.wait

* Initial plan

* Replace Thread.delay with Unix.sleepf in eval to fix hang in Domain-spawned threads

Thread.delay depends on OCaml's threads library per-domain state
(caml_release_domain_lock/caml_acquire_domain_lock), which may not be
properly initialized for domains created via Domain.spawn on Windows
and macOS. Unix.sleepf uses the OCaml runtime's blocking section API
(caml_enter_blocking_section/caml_leave_blocking_section) which works
correctly for any domain.

Also simplified Sys.sleep to avoid mixing OCaml's Sys.time() (CPU time)
with wall-clock sleep durations, and added safety guards for negative
sleep values.

Co-authored-by: Simn <634365+Simn@users.noreply.github.com>
